figures.caption: Figure 2: Overview of MemDLM. Left: standard MDLM training uses a static single-step denoising objective from xt to x0. Right: MemDLM uses Bi-level Optimization in which an inner loop updates fast weights ϕ along an anchor-consistent local trajectory (xtpre → xt → x0), and the outer loop updates the base model θ on the anchor state xt conditioned on this parametric memory. Legend: dark tokens denote mask tokens, light tokens denote observed tokens, straight arrows denote forward or reverse prediction flow, and blue curved arrows denote inner-loop fast-weight updates.
